Part B.key_evidence: Single first detection (ndethist=1) with negative subtraction (isdiffpos=f) in g-band at magpsf~19.8. The cutouts are dominated by noisy/striped patterns with no clean point-like residual at center; classtar is low (0.265) and FWHM is ~4.2 px (somewhat broad). Nearest PS1 source is 13.8 arcsec away (sgscore1~0.78) so it is not a relevant host. ncovhist is high (1049) yet only one detection ever recorded at this position.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: The combination of a noisy/striped difference image, no clear central PSF-like residual, low classtar, and a single detection at a position covered ~1049 times strongly suggests this is an image-subtraction artifact (bogus) rather than a true astrophysical transient.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative would be a faint real variable/SN with a poor-quality detection, but the absence of a coherent point source in science and difference, plus no nearby host (PS1 neighbor 13.8 arcsec away), makes a real astrophysical source unlikely.
